checks.a_analysis: I have reviewed the output against the source document. Every heading and text block in the output, including the 'Framework Layers' and 'Other Important Layers' table contents, is derived directly from the source text on Page 1 and Page 2. There are no hallucinated paragraphs, introductions, or conclusions.
The source document contains no phone numbers, email addresses, or URLs. The output correctly reflects this absence and does not invent any contact information.

The source document consists of two pages. Page 1 contains the 'Framework Layers' and the beginning of 'Other Important Layers'. Page 2 contains the conclusion of the 'Other Important Layers' table. Both sections are present in the output JSON across two separate section objects.

I counted the rows in both the source and output. Page 1 source has 9 Framework rows and 18 'Other' rows. Page 2 source has 10 'Other' rows. The output JSON preserves all of these rows across two tables. The split of the 'Wetlands' description across the page break is handled by creating two entries, preserving the full text content.

Minor structural differences, such as the 'Wetlands' description being split between two tables due to a page break, are acceptable reformatting and do not change the meaning of the information provided.

checks.a_analysis: The document title 'NC OneMap Implementation: Initial Data Layers to Serve' appears twice in the output (as a heading for each section). However, this mirrors the source document which includes this title as a header on both Page 1 and Page 2. This is intentional repetition in the source and is not a defect.

The output uses H1 for the page titles/headers. There are no other headings used (H2, H3, etc.). While the table contains section breaks like 'Other Important Layers', rendering these as table rows instead of promoting them to headings is an acceptable structural choice that preserves the grid layout. There are no skipped heading levels.

The table structure is corrupted due to the page split. On Page 1, the last row is 'Wetlands', but its description is cut off ('Wetlands areas from the US Fish and'). On Page 2, the remaining text of that description ('Wildlife Service, National Wetlands Inventory') is rendered as a single-cell header row for a new table. This is a structural corruption because: 1) A single row of data from the source is split across two separate table structures in the output. 2) Data (the end of a description) is misidentified as a header row in the second table. 3) The resulting second table has inconsistent column counts, starting with a 1-column header followed by 2-column data rows.
checks.b_evidence_source: Wetlands | Wetlands areas from the US Fish and [Page 1] ... [Page 2] Wildlife Service, National Wetlands Inventory
checks.b_evidence_output: Table 1 (reading_order 2) ends with 'Wetlands'. Table 2 (reading_order 5) starts with: { "cell_type": "header", "content": [ { "text": "Wildlife Service, National Wetlands Inventory" } ], "colspan": 1, "rowspan": 1, "headers": null }

checks.a_analysis: The reading order follows the logical flow of the source document from top to bottom, including the repeated page headers.
The source document does not contain formatted lists; all information is presented within a table structure.
There are no links or URLs present in the source document.

checks.a_analysis: The HTML output generally preserves the visual organization of the source PDF. It maintains a two-column table structure and reflects the page breaks by repeating the document title before the second part of the table, which matches the layout of the original two-page PDF. The addition of a technical header ('Appenc Initialdatalayers 685Fad7D') is a minor stylistic difference and does not interfere with the organization of the content.

There is a significant rendering error in the table representing the second page of the PDF. In the source PDF, the first row of page 2 contains a continuation of the 'Wetlands' description in the right-hand column ('Wildlife Service, National Wetlands Inventory'), with the left-hand column remaining empty. In the HTML output, this text is incorrectly placed in the first column, while the second column is left empty. This misrendering changes the meaning of the data, making a fragment of a description appear as a new layer name.
checks.b_evidence_source: Page 2 of the PDF shows a table row where the left cell is empty and the right cell contains 'Wildlife Service, National Wetlands Inventory'.
checks.b_evidence_output: The HTML screenshot for the second page section shows the first row of the table containing 'Wildlife Service, National Wetlands Inventory' in the first column and an empty second column.

checks.a_analysis: The source PDF is entirely text-based with tables and contains no meaningful images, logos, or diagrams. Therefore, no images are expected in the output.

The document title 'NC OneMap Implementation: Initial Data Layers to Serve' appears twice in the HTML, but this is not an error as it reflects the source PDF where the title is present at the top of both page 1 and page 2. No other body content, paragraphs, or table data are duplicated.
